bugs_found.description: signNoZero<float> returns 0 for NaN, violates ne_zero.
negate<int16_t> is not a global involution. negate(negate(-32767))=-32768. INT16_MAX special case wrong.

ci_status: healthy - toolchain pinned v4.29.0, cache on lake-manifest
run16_tasks: Task 2 (expo informal spec) + Task 3 (expo Lean spec: 15 proofs, 1 sorry for expo_in_range)
notes: set tactic NOT available in Lean 4 stdlib (it's in Mathlib/Std) - avoid it
norm_num tactic NOT available in Lean 4 stdlib (unreachable if simp closes goal first)
linarith NOT available, ring NOT available, ring_nf NOT available
For Rat subtraction: use Rat.sub_eq_add_neg + Rat.neg_zero + Rat.add_zero to prove a-0=a
Rat.neg_add: -(a+b) = -a + -b (both implicit args). rw [<- Rat.neg_add] rewrites -a+-b to -(a+b)
Rat.le_iff_lt_or_eq.mp: a <= b -> a < b | a = b (useful for case splits on clamped boundaries)
Rat.neg_le_iff.mp rfl: proves -1 <= 1
Rat.not_lt.mpr rfl: proves various ¬(a < b) for specific values (e.g. ¬0>1, ¬1<-1)
constrainRat neg_symm: use Rat.neg_lt_neg h + rw [Rat.neg_neg], Rat.neg_le_neg
expo_in_range sorry: needs mul_self_le_one for x^2<=1 when 0<=x<=1
Rat.sub_add_cancel/Rat.sub_self: IMPLICIT args - use bare (no obvious args)
Rat.add_le_add_right: shared term on RIGHT (a+c <= b+c). For left-shared: add_comm first.
x^0=1: use 'have h0 := by simp' (simp knows pow_zero)
AlphaFilter ge_sample fix: use Rat.neg_le_neg on (alpha*(state-sample) <= state-sample), then rewrite -(alpha*(state-sample)) = alpha*(sample-state) using <- Rat.mul_neg + Rat.neg_sub
AlphaFilter iterate_formula fix: use 'induction n generalizing state' (NOT plain induction)
CI at .github/workflows/lean-ci.yml - working, lake update step added run12
WelfordMean mean_step_algebra: need (n+1) NOT n+1 in function def.
succ_cast_ne_zero: exact_mod_cast Nat.succ_ne_zero n works for ((n+1):Rat) <> 0
push_cast; rfl works for (n+1) = n + 1 in Rat
Rat.inv_mul_cancel (not div_mul_cancel): rw [Rat.div_def, Rat.mul_assoc, Rat.inv_mul_cancel _ hn, Rat.mul_one]
lerp_half sorry: needs explicit Rat 1/2 = (1:Rat)/(2:Rat) inv arithmetic.
